note: Machine-readable genre constraints consumed by gen.py (generation bias/filter), the interpretation hook (lens), tick (world escalation), and worldgen (defaults). Genre is weighted bias + a small deny-list, not a wall — Fabula Ultima explicitly invites the odd-one-out hero. 'core' is the neutral default when no Atlas is chosen.

genres.name: Fabula Ultima (neutral)

pillars.name: It's about the heroes
pillars.text: The Player Characters are the protagonists; the world reacts to their Bonds, Themes, and choices.

pillars.name: Bonds drive the story
pillars.text: Relationships (admiration/inferiority, loyalty/mistrust, affection/hatred) are the engine of drama and are invoked mechanically.

pillars.name: Hope against despair
pillars.text: Hardship feeds resolve (Fabula Points) which feeds growth; defeat is real but not the end.

pillars.name: A world of wonder
pillars.text: Magic, myth, and spectacle in a JRPG register; the world hides secrets worth uncovering.

conflict_themes.value: a rising power threatens the region
a monster or villain terrorizes the innocent
an ancient secret resurfaces with a price
a personal betrayal or lost bond returns
a community is torn between two hard choices

genres.item_flavor: classic fantasy arms, relics, and potions

genres.name: High Fantasy

pillars.name: The tip of the iceberg
pillars.text: The world hides incredible secrets and powerful magic; today's Villains are often pawns in a much vaster game revealed as the campaign delves deeper.

pillars.name: Warring nations & magitech empires
pillars.text: Empires, kingdoms, and orders clash; alone, few peoples can stand against an expansionist magitech power.

pillars.name: Awakening ancient powers
pillars.text: Elemental Guardians, sacred flames, astral drakes, and world-shaping relics slumber until crisis wakes them.

pillars.name: Heroes of destiny
pillars.text: Unlikely and chosen heroes alike are pulled into legends far larger than themselves.

conflict_themes.value: an empire's magitech army invades a weaker neighbor
an ancient power (guardian, drake, sacred flame) is stirring
monster hordes surge from rifts opened by a hidden hand
magic is outlawed or hoarded by a ruling caste
reacting relics (Light Stone / Shadow Stone) threaten catastrophe
a pilgrimage or quest demands a terrible sacrifice

genres.item_flavor: enchanted arms, elemental stones, holy relics, magitech armor

genres.name: Natural Fantasy

pillars.name: Small-scale conflict
pillars.text: Clashes are intimate: tensions between neighboring villages or clans, humans against animals or spirits, or feuds within one people.

pillars.name: Nature, seasons & spirits
pillars.text: The land lives — ancient trees, seasonal essences, and guardian fairies; harm to nature is harm to all.

pillars.name: Survival, herbalism & craft
pillars.text: Herbalism, medicine, cooking, foraging, and camp life matter as much as combat.

pillars.name: From cozy to dark
pillars.text: Tone ranges from gentle and pastoral to expropriation, violence, and even genocide in its darkest forms.

conflict_themes.value: two villages or clans feud over land, water, or a sacred site
a wilderness is dying or blighted; its spirit begs for aid
a nature spirit or guardian has been angered or corrupted
the balance of a season is broken
outsiders expropriate or exploit a natural community
a beloved mentor's illness needs a rare natural cure

genres.item_flavor: herbal remedies, foraged materials, hand-crafted tools, seasonal charms

pillars.name: Ancient ruins & harsh lands
pillars.text: Wondrous ruins and hostile environments hide lost technology and government experiments.

pillars.name: A world in peril
pillars.text: Ecological collapse, orbital weapons, and reckless powers push whole planets toward ruin.

pillars.name: Clashing communities
pillars.text: Corporations, federations, protectorates, and rebels grind against each other for control and survival.

pillars.name: Everything has a soul
pillars.text: Psychospace and the soul-network let thought travel as data — and let thoughts become very real demons.

pillars.name: Magic and technology
pillars.text: Magitech blends spellcraft with machines; the two are not opposites but tools of the same world.

pillars.name: Heroes of many shapes
pillars.text: Heroes are humans, aliens, uplifted animals, constructs — bound by cause, not kind.

conflict_themes.value: a corporation exploits workers or a world for profit
a soul-network / AI node goes rogue and gathers followers
an orbital or military power imposes tyranny by force
a government experiment breaks containment
extraction strips a planet of its resources or souls
psychospace demons spill into the real world
